articles.full_text:
- Rajasthan Thunderstorms & Hailstorms Today | Heatwave Relief Till April 10
Rajasthan braces for stormy weather:Thunderstorms and hailstorms likely today; heatwave relief expected till 10 April
- Copy link
People in Rajasthan will not have to endure intense heat and heatwave until April 10. Due to back-to-back western disturbances coming to the state, the spell of rain, storms, and hailstorms will likely continue till April 7-8.
The first system of April became active from Thursday. Due to its effect, the weather changed in areas of Sri Ganganagar, Hanumangarh, Jaisalmer, Barmer, and Bikaner. There was stormy rain in many parts of Sri Ganganagar and Hanumangarh, and hail fell at some places.
Orange alert issued for 8 districts today
Weather experts said, “The maximum impact of this system will be seen on April 3. An orange alert has been issued for storms, rain, and hailstorms in a total of 8 districts on April 3. There is a yellow alert in all other districts. It will weaken after April 4. From the evening of April 6, another new system will become active in Rajasthan".
Radheshyam Sharma, Director of the Meteorological Center Jaipur, said, “The system coming from April 6 will also be strong, and due to its effect, thunderstorm activities (storms, lightning, hailstorms) along with light to moderate rain may be witnessed in many parts of the state on April 7-8”.
Damage to Crops
Looking at the weather of the last 24 hours, it rained in the areas of Sriganganagar, Hanumangarh, Bikaner, and Churu. Strong winds blew in many places in these areas and stormy rain was witnessed. Hailstones fell in the areas of Padampur and Anupgarh in Sriganganagar.
This change in weather has provided relief from the heat. On the other hand, farmers have suffered losses. Ripe wheat crops have fallen in the fields in the areas of Sriganganagar and Hanumangarh.
Kota records highest temperature
On Thursday, Kota recorded the highest temperature in the state. The maximum temperature here was recorded at 38.3 degrees Celsius. The sky remained clear in Kota with bright sunshine during the day. The weather remained clear in Jaipur as well on Thursday. The maximum temperature here was recorded at 36 degrees Celsius.
Similarly, the maximum temperature was 37.6 in Chittorgarh, 37.9 in Churu, 37.6 in Sriganganagar, 37.7 in Fatehpur (Sikar), 37.2 in Dausa, 36.3 in Bikaner, 35 in Udaipur, 35.4 in Alwar, 35.2 in Pilani (Jhunjhunu), 35.8 in Vanasthali (Tonk), 34.8 in Jodhpur, 34.3 in Barmer, 34 in Phalodi and 34.6 degrees Celsius in Nagaur.
New System from April 6
Radheshyam Sharma, Director of the Meteorological Center Jaipur, said, “Currently one weather system is active in the state, while the second system will become active from April 6. Both these systems will have a significant impact”.
He added, “This will bring rainfall to many districts of the state. There is no possibility of a heatwave in the state till April 10. The temperature is also likely to remain below 40 degrees Celsius”.
